definitions: Relating to or denoting a Bronze Age civilization in Crete (c. 2700–1450 BC), characterized by the development of a script (Linear A), elaborate palaces, and a sophisticated art style.
A person belonging to the Minoan civilization.
examples: The Minoan civilization flourished on Crete.
Minoan art is known for its vibrant frescoes.
Archaeologists have uncovered many Minoan artifacts.
